purpose: Reusable implementation context for fixing sync-agents idempotency.
Avoids rediscovering root cause, verification commands, and repo invariants before implementation/DevOps steps.
applies_to: sync-agents GitHub workflow dispatch and remote result sync
non_goals: Do not redesign Gem Team agent sync contract.
Do not add provider/model routing or hardcoded model IDs.
Do not update PRD unless implementation changes product behavior.

tech_stack.name: Node.js ESM
tech_stack.version: 22 in GitHub workflow; local version from environment

tech_stack.name: TypeScript
tech_stack.usage_context: src and test compilation before node:test execution

tech_stack.name: GitHub Actions / gh CLI
tech_stack.usage_context: manual sync action trigger and remote result retrieval

conventions: Build command is named compile, not build, to avoid npm git-dependency preparation hooks.
npm test runs npm run compile first, then node --test dist/test/**/*.test.js.
Generated artifacts under agents/generated and dist must remain aligned with validation scripts.
Docs/PRD.md is the product source of truth; trust code/tests plus PRD when docs diverge.

hard: When upstream stable content is unchanged, sync-agents must not touch agents/generated/manifest.json or generated agent bodies.
When upstream content changes, sync-agents must still rewrite generated output and remove obsolete generated files.
Do not let generated defaults override existing user agent.model.
Do not let gem-orchestrator route to itself.
Do not add role_models, agent_complexity_models, per-agent model overrides, provider-side routing, or concrete model IDs.
T4 DevOps must use one consistent workflow_ref for commit, push, workflow_dispatch, and pull; default workflow_ref is the current branch/ref after the fix commit.
Before gh workflow run sync-agents.yml --ref <workflow_ref>, the remote workflow_ref must be verified to contain the fix commit; otherwise the workflow may run stale code.
soft: Keep changes limited to sync script, tests, and generated artifacts only when necessary.
Prefer no real network in unit tests; use fixture/fetch/IO injection or temp dirs.
compatibility: Manifest must keep upstream source URL, commit, body hash, local slug, syncedAt, and syncBatchId fields for validation compatibility.
No-op comparison should ignore volatile syncedAt/syncBatchId but preserve them unchanged from previous manifest.
If sync-agents workflow is only meaningful on main, using main requires explicit user approval for direct main push/merge when branch policy is unknown.
security_requirements: No secrets, tokens, cookies, API keys, private keys, or provider credentials in code/docs/generated files.
GitHub workflow uses github.token; local gh auth issues are execution blockers, not code assumptions.

scripts/: validate-sync.mjs validates generated manifest and bodies
test/: TypeScript node:test suites compiled into dist/test
src/sync/: runtime TypeScript validation types and manifest validation

patterns: EXPECTED_SLUGS/CANONICAL_GEM_TEAM_SLUGS define the canonical 16-agent list in sync and validation layers.
Validation checks forbidden model/config dimensions and secret-like assignments in manifest/body content.
Workflow commits only when git diff --cached is non-empty after sync/validation/tests.

key_components.name: sync-agents script
responsibility: Resolve upstream ref to commit via GitHub API.
Generate agents/generated/*.agent.md and manifest.json with hashes/timestamps/batch metadata.

key_components.name: generated manifest
responsibility: Records source owner/repo/path/ref/commit, expected slugs, per-agent URLs/hash/bytes/metadata, syncedAt, syncBatchId.
Currently contains upstream ref gem-team-v1.66.0 and commit 716fc1e77a2f4faf620172ee57d25906bfd5552c.

key_components.name: sync workflow
responsibility: Resolves latest upstream release tag with gh api.
Runs npm run sync:agents, validation/tests/lint/dist validation, then commits generated changes only when diff exists.

key_components.name: manifest validation
responsibility: Ensures canonical slugs, upstream metadata, body hashes, no orchestrator self-routing, no forbidden model/config dimensions, and no secret-like assignments.

purpose: Fetch and generate Gem Team agents
why_relevant: Root cause is unconditional rm/rewrite and fresh timestamp/syncBatchId generation on every run.

key_elements.description: Volatile values created before any change detection.
Unconditionally deletes generated output before knowing whether stable content changed.
Always writes manifest with new syncedAt/syncBatchId.

why_relevant: After local fix, user requires triggering this workflow and pulling remote result locally.

key_elements.description: Manual trigger used by gh workflow run sync-agents.yml --ref main.
Commits only when staged diff exists; no-op script should make unchanged upstream workflow produce no commit.

why_relevant: The file that must not change when upstream stable content is unchanged.

key_elements.description: Current baseline is gem-team-v1.66.0 at commit 716fc1e77a2f4faf620172ee57d25906bfd5552c.
Stable per-agent fields used to detect real upstream changes.

why_relevant: Defines required npm scripts and npm test behavior.

key_elements.description: compile, typecheck, test, lint, sync:agents, validate:sync, validate:dist, validate:no-model-ids.

internal: src/sync/validation.ts -> src/index.js exports used by tests
external: GitHub API commits endpoint for mubaidr/gem-team
gh CLI for workflow dispatch/result inspection

gotchas.text: Do not compare JSON-serialized manifest including syncedAt/syncBatchId; those fields are intentionally volatile on real updates but must remain unchanged on no-op.

No-op cannot skip fetching bodies solely because upstreamCommit matches; tags/refs and metadata/routing target extraction should still be validated through proposed stable entries or carefully justified cache semantics.
Unit tests should not rely on live GitHub because network/tag state can make idempotency tests flaky.
After external file edits, run validation commands; if using IDE semantic results later, sync IDE cache first.
Do not commit/push a fix on a non-main branch and then trigger gh workflow run sync-agents.yml --ref main; the workflow ref must already contain the fix commit.

security_considerations.concern: Reject forbidden model/config dimensions, concrete provider model IDs, and secret-like assignments.
Uses GitHub token in action; local execution should not expose or write credentials.

testing_patterns.framework: node:test with assert/strict, TypeScript compiled before execution
mock_patterns: Use temp dirs and injected dependencies for script behavior; avoid live network in unit tests.

domain_context.error_handling: Scripts throw or collect failures, print concise failure messages, and exit non-zero for validation failures.
domain_context.data_flow: sync-agents resolves upstream ref -> fetches 16 raw bodies -> hashes/extracts metadata -> writes agents/generated bodies and manifest -> validate-sync verifies metadata/body hashes -> workflow commits only if git diff exists.

open_questions.question: Is gh CLI authenticated with permission to push the selected current workflow_ref, trigger sync-agents.yml on that ref, and read the run result?
open_questions.context: Required for final workflow dispatch and remote result sync; T4 must verify remote workflow_ref contains the fix commit before dispatch. If the action is only meaningful on main, direct main push/merge requires explicit user approval when policy is unknown.

prior_decisions.decision: Fix should be a no-op gate in sync-agents rather than workflow-only filtering.
rationale: The script itself currently causes manifest-only diffs by regenerating volatile values and rewriting output.
Local sync command should also be idempotent, not just scheduled CI.
linked_constraints: No manifest update when upstream stable content is unchanged

prior_decisions.decision: PRD update is not recommended unless implementation changes product behavior.
rationale: This is a maintenance/idempotency bug fix within existing sync contract.
PRD already requires sync metadata and validation, not a new user-facing feature.
linked_constraints: Source of truth docs/PRD.md; update docs only if product behavior changes

prior_decisions.decision: T4 uses the current branch/ref as workflow_ref for commit, push, workflow dispatch, and pull unless explicit user approval is obtained to move the fix onto main.
rationale: Reviewer found the prior plan could push a fix to a non-main branch but trigger workflow on main, causing the action to miss the fix commit.
Verifying the remote workflow_ref contains HEAD before dispatch ensures GitHub Actions runs the fixed workflow/code state.
linked_constraints: commit/push/workflow/pull must use a single selected workflow_ref
